I was always curious to visit this museum. This museum was found within Lower Manhattan. It had a restaurant on its ground floor, but I did not have a dining experience herein. It cost ten dollars ($10.00) as an entry price to see this museum, and I spent thirty (30) minutes at this museum’s several stories. I liked the various artworks and artifacts. As an original inhabitant of Fort Lee, New Jersey, I enjoyed the ability to learn about the Revolutionary War history of my hometown.

This was on my Must-See List, and I wasn't disappointed.
We went for the Museum, not the tavern.
We know next to nothing about its history, except for knowing George Washington is connected to it. But if you make sure you are there for the short guided tour, it doesn't matter that you don't know much. The history is brilliant, and I'm sure all the guides are as knowledgeable as the man we had.
Definitely a highlight of our visit to NYC.

Loved the opportunity to have a great meal that was reasonably priced in this historic building.  Lovely atmosphere, attentive staff, great options for everyone in our group (including a vegetarian).  We had a leisurely meal, then went upstairs to the reasonably priced museum which we enjoyed immensely.  History nerds and/or people who like good food will enjoy this place.
